transcript_facts:
*   The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
*   The patient denies blood in stool.
*   The clinician suspects cervical radiculopathy.
*   The clinician plans a cervical MRI.
*   The patient reports pain on the right hand side of the belly.
*   The patient reports the pain feels like a squeezing severe pain.
*   The patient reports the pain comes and goes, but when it comes it’s severe.
*   The patient rates the pain as an eight.
*   The patient states the pain comes immediately after eating or two hours after eating.
*   The patient states he likes to eat.
*   The patient reports his symptoms improved when he cut back and went more of a keto diet.
*   The patient reports the fatty food was difficult to cut out.
*   The patient reports he still gets pain.
*   The patient states he has a family history of gallbladder disease.
*   The patient states both his parents had their gallbladders taken out.
*   The patient states he is not exercising much.
*   The patient enjoys photography and nature.
*   The patient mentions he has nature pictures.
*   The patient states he has had low grade fevers.
*   The patient states he has not vomited.
*   The doctor notes positive bowel sounds.
*   The doctor notes slight guarding to the right upper quadrant.
*   The doctor notes no rebound tenderness.
*   The doctor notes Murphy’s signs.
*   The ultrasound shows multiple gallstones in the gallbladder.
*   The common bile duct size is within normal limits.
*   The doctor recommends a laparoscopic cholecystectomy.
*   The surgery will take about an hour to an hour and a half.
*   The patient will likely spend the night in the hospital.
*   The patient should avoid high fat foods after surgery.
*   The patient will need to follow up with his physician regarding his diabetes.
